attributes.description: Alerts when `iis.request.rejected` increases. Rejections mean IIS turned away requests because the queue was full or limits were reached.
attributes.name: [IIS OTel] Request rejections detected

investigation_guide.blob:
## IIS request rejections detected

### What fired
`INCREASE(iis.request.rejected) > 0` on a pool during the window.

### Why it matters
Rejected requests are immediate HTTP 503 failures returned to the client without ever being processed. Almost always indicates a saturated pool — either the request queue is full or an explicit limit (`maxConcurrentRequestsPerCPU`, `processModel.requestQueueLimit`) was reached.

### Triage
1. Identify the affected pool.
2. Inspect queue depth and queue age trends.
3. Check `w3wp.exe` health (CPU, threads, GC) on the host.
4. Look at downstream call latency.

### Remediation
- Raise the queue limit if hardware allows.
- Add worker processes.
- Fix slow downstream calls.

### Tuning
- Fires on any non-zero increase. Each rejection is a client-visible 503 — do not loosen the threshold without good reason.

esqlQuery.esql:
TS metrics-iisreceiver.otel-*
| WHERE iis.request.rejected IS NOT NULL
| STATS rejected_increase = SUM(INCREASE(iis.request.rejected)) BY iis.application_pool, host.name
| WHERE rejected_increase > 0
| SORT rejected_increase DESC
| LIMIT 20
